raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The Assembly Higher Education and Veterans Affairs Committees held a hearing to examine ways to better protect student veterans affected by for-profit educational institution closures.",
    "California's current policies intended to help students recover economic losses due to institution closures are weak.",
    "AB 1916 requires for-profit schools to post a surety bond covering the amount of tuition and fees paid by students for an academic period.",
    "In the event of a closure, the Bureau for Private Postsecondary Education (BPPE) can demand the surety bond to administer refunds to students.",
    "The growth of for-profit campuses in the last decade has created challenges for regulators overseeing these institutions.",
    "Many for-profit schools provide access to vocational training and flexibility for non-traditional students where gaps exist in the higher education system.",
    "Nearly 1000 private postsecondary institutions operate in California, many of which are small and financially vulnerable.",
    "Revenue from public financial aid is crucial for many for-profit institutions, and interruption of this funding can lead to their closure.",
    "The closure of Corinthian Colleges in 2015 exemplifies the risk of financial instability affecting for-profit schools.",
    "Many for-profit campuses employ aggressive recruitment tactics targeting veterans and working professionals.",
    "40 states require for-profit schools to post a surety bond as collateral in the event of closure, protecting students from financial loss.",
    "California’s students contribute to the Student Tuition Recovery Fund (STRF), a self-insurance fund that reimburses tuition upon closure.",
    "AB 1916 mandates that the surety bond posted by an institution must reimburse students before the STRF is utilized."
  ]
}
```
